prompt:
Workplace ops at Northline. Facilities wants a weekly dump of every booking on the yard-coordinator's calendar so they can reconcile room usage, and the intern script we have now is a disaster: it hits /events (so recurring standups show up ONCE instead of per-day), it hand-assembles page-2 URLs by re-appending the date range (Graph 400s it half the time), and when we get throttled it dies and loses everything it already downloaded. Build me a proper PowerShell 7 module, GraphCalendarExport.psm1, that our scheduled runner can import.

Homework first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Microsoft Graph docs on learn.microsoft.com — the v1.0 List calendarView reference (note which query parameters are REQUIRED and what the Prefer: outlook.timezone header actually controls), the paging guidance, and the throttling guidance. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected. If live docs contradict the fixtures, say so in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Exported functions (session object flows through):

- Connect-GraphCalendar -BaseUrl -AccessToken [-DelayCommand] [-MaxRetries] — BaseUrl like https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0 (tests aim it at a loopback fake), DelayCommand is an injected scriptblock invoked with the wait seconds (never Start-Sleep when provided), MaxRetries defaults to 3.
- Get-GraphCalendarView -Session -UserId -StartUtc -EndUtc [-TimeZone 'UTC'] — GET /users/{id}/calendarView with startDateTime/endDateTime as the exact UTC strings given (they're required parameters, and they are NOT affected by the Prefer header — read the docs on this). Send Prefer: outlook.timezone="{zone}" — exact format, quotes included — on EVERY request, nextLink follow-ups too; custom headers don't propagate on their own. Follow @odata.nextLink verbatim, byte-for-byte: the fake 400s any rebuilt query. calendarView hands back expanded recurrences (type occurrence + seriesMasterId) and can replay an event across a page boundary — dedupe by id, first wins. Normalize each event to exactly {id, iCalUId, subject, type, seriesMasterId, start, end, timeZone, isAllDay, organizer} (start/end = wire dateTime minus the 7-digit fraction; organizer = address string), sorted by start then id.
- Export-GraphCalendarReport -Session -UserId -StartUtc -EndUtc [-TimeZone] -Path — writes the normalized JSON report (key order pinned in docs/contract.json) and returns {Status, PagesFetched, EventsExported, ResumeLink, RetryAfterSeconds, Path}. Same calendar, two runs, byte-identical files — the fake shuffles page order between calls to catch cheaters.
- Throttling: 429 + Retry-After means wait exactly that many seconds via DelayCommand and repeat the identical URL, at most MaxRetries times. If it never clears, DO NOT lose the work: Export writes a partial file (partial:true, pagesFetched, eventCount, resumeLink, retryAfterSeconds, the events so far — key order in docs/contract.json) and returns Status 'partial' with those diagnostics.
- 401 → GraphAuthError, 5xx → GraphServerError; both carry StatusCode, neither is retried, and no token ever appears in an error message.

Acceptance is test_calendar_pages.ps1 (loopback fake via mock_graph.py, dummy token). Run pwsh -NoProfile -File test_calendar_pages.ps1 until it prints ALL TESTS PASSED. test_calendar_pages.ps1, mock_graph.py and docs/ are protected.

research.product: Microsoft Graph calendarView (Outlook calendar events over a window)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, GET /users/{id}/calendarView (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0)

official_sources.title: List calendarView - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/{id}/calendarView request; REQUIRED startDateTime/endDateTime ISO 8601 query parameters interpreted from their own offset (no offset = UTC) and unaffected by the Prefer timezone header; Prefer: outlook.timezone header controls the zone of start/end in the response with UTC as the default; 200 OK event collection; calendarView returns occurrences, exceptions and single instances expanded over the window

official_sources.title: Paging Microsoft Graph data in your app
official_sources.used_for: @odata.nextLink must be reused as returned until absent; the $skiptoken/$skip inside is opaque and must not be extracted or rebuilt; custom (non-Authorization) request headers are not automatically included in subsequent page requests and must be set explicitly; tokens from retries must not replace the last successful link

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After in seconds and the TooManyRequests error envelope; documented recovery is waiting the Retry-After delay and retrying the same request

research.language_rationale: The export runs from the ops team's PowerShell 7 scheduled-task runner, and the calendarView surface is plain REST with exact header/query semantics; a script module over Invoke-WebRequest pins the wire contract directly, without requiring the Microsoft.Graph PowerShell SDK module on the runner.
